page: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS-family high-performance computing instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8168 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 44 dedicated physical cores and 44 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, with no hyperthreading per core. It features 352.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 717 GB of local storage, supports Premium Storage, and is RDMA-capable for high-throughput, low-latency networking.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The HC44rs achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU stress tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation tasks. Its memory architecture delivers high read bandwidth for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks, which is beneficial for memory-bound workloads. Additionally, the instance exhibits strong performance in Redis SET operations. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with average single-core CPU scores and weak single-threaded gzip compression throughput. Large language model text generation speeds are also limited, reflecting the lack of dedicated hardware acceleration.

Given its high memory density and dedicated physical cores, the HC44rs offers high resource density for memory-intensive and parallel computing tasks without the overhead of hyperthreading. The inclusion of RDMA networking and local SSD storage makes it a cost-efficient choice for clustered high-performance computing simulations, large-scale database operations, and memory-bound scientific workloads. It is well-suited for parallel processing, software compilation pipelines, and in-memory data stores, though it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or heavy AI inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS-family high-performance computing instance designed for memory-intensive and parallel workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8168 processor on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 44 dedicated physical cores and 44 vCPUs at 2.7 GHz, paired with 352.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and 717 GB of local storage. The instance is RDMA-capable and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory read bandwidth, and excellent throughput in software compilation and Redis operations. However, single-threaded performance is average, and text generation for large language models is poor due to the absence of a GPU. The HC44rs is highly suited for clustered HPC simulations, large-scale databases, and parallel processing workloads where high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores are critical.
og_description: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS-family instance featuring 44 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 352 GB of RAM, and RDMA networking, optimized for memory-intensive parallel computing and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS-family instance with 44 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 352 GB RAM, and RDMA networking for parallel computing.
tagline: A dedicated 44-core high-memory instance with RDMA networking designed for parallel computing, database operations, and memory-bound scientific workloads.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores with no hyperthreading
352 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
RDMA-capable networking and 717 GB of local storage
Strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance
Best suited for clustered HPC, databases, and parallel processing
